rationale: Wosinska is cited repeatedly in the main text, and the authors summarize her specific finding that competitor DTCA can raise category compliance while own-brand DTCA can lower compliance. They use this evidence to motivate their hypotheses that advertising effects on compliance may be mixed in sign, and later cite it again when interpreting broader DTCA results. This is more than a passing mention because it helps frame the advertising mechanism and the paper's positioning on why opposing DTCA views can both appear valid.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Wosinska (2005) as prior empirical evidence to frame its DTCA-compliance hypotheses and to interpret mixed advertising effects. It situates its own individual-level, multi-category heterogeneity analysis as a broader explanation of those mixed effects.
